item.title: I feel guilty when I ask Claude to do something I could have done myself (but would have taken me mu...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
I feel guilty when I ask Claude to do something I could have done myself (but would have taken me much longer). Claude is so busy, I hate to be annoying. I suppose I'll get over it.

John Preskill

item.title: That feeling when you meet someone for the first time with whom you have already co-authored a paper...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
That feeling when you meet someone for the first time with whom you have already co-authored a paper.

John Preskill

item.title: [John Preskill 已转帖] Today, Caltech and JPL welcome renowned astrophysicist, acclaimed author, and transformative leader,...
item.author: Caltech
item.content:encoded:
Today, Caltech and JPL welcome renowned astrophysicist, acclaimed author, and transformative leader, Ray Jayawardhana as the Institute’s tenth president! He brings a fierce commitment to building on Caltech’s legacy of discovery and exploration. https://tinyurl.com/5y3j6ebx

Caltech

item.title: [John Preskill 已转帖] Ginsburg Center for Quantum Precision Measurement Construction Timelapse
item.author: Caltech
item.content:encoded:
Ginsburg Center for Quantum Precision Measurement Construction Timelapse

Caltech

item.title: On Wednesday we dedicated @Caltech's Ginsburg Center for Quantum Precision Measurement. We won’t be...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
On Wednesday we dedicated @Caltech's Ginsburg Center for Quantum Precision Measurement. We won’t be moving in for a few more months, but it’s going to be a beautiful facility. We’re very grateful to the donors, and to President Tom Rosenbaum and former Division Chair Fiona

John Preskill

item.title: My friend Charles Cao taught me about the importance of magic in quantum spacetime: "Without magic,...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
My friend Charles Cao taught me about the importance of magic in quantum spacetime: "Without magic, things are a little too simple." Thanks, @walkingthedot, for the well-written article.

John Preskill

item.title: We thought that @SenAdamSchiff would want to hear about how quantum computing will benefit the world...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
We thought that @SenAdamSchiff would want to hear about how quantum computing will benefit the world. But actually he is just really, really curious about quantum entanglement (and knows a lot about it)! Thanks for visiting @Caltech, Senator. It was fun.

John Preskill

item.title: Even on the bad days, there are good things to celebrate and enjoy.
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
Even on the bad days, there are good things to celebrate and enjoy.

John Preskill

item.title: I'm quite amazed, and deeply honored, to see my name on this distinguished list of "AI Pioneers." Th...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
I'm quite amazed, and deeply honored, to see my name on this distinguished list of "AI Pioneers." Thank you, @BostonGF and AI World Society, for recognizing that quantum science can contribute to "an age of human flourishing." https://bostonglobalforum.org/publication/16058/…

John Preskill

item.title: From dream to reality: The bridge connecting quantum information and matter to high energy physics @...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
From dream to reality: The bridge connecting quantum information and matter to high energy physics @Caltech now exists.

John Preskill

item.title: Everybody loves entropy, yet somehow we are always working hard to reduce it.
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
Everybody loves entropy, yet somehow we are always working hard to reduce it.

John Preskill

item.title: Wise words from Garnet Chan on quantum computing and chemistry.
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
Wise words from Garnet Chan on quantum computing and chemistry.

John Preskill

item.title: Our paper “Exponential Quantum Advantage in Processing Massive Classical Data” proves that quantum m...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
Our paper “Exponential Quantum Advantage in Processing Massive Classical Data” proves that quantum machines can solve common machine learning tasks using exponentially less memory than a classical machine would require. Much further work will be needed to translate this theory

John Preskill

item.title: [John Preskill 已转帖] ⚛ Can small quantum computers accelerate AI on massive classical data? Yes! I am absolutely thrilled...
item.author: Haimeng Zhao
item.content:encoded:
⚛ Can small quantum computers accelerate AI on massive classical data? Yes! I am absolutely thrilled to share our new work proving *honest* exponential quantum advantages in broadly applicable classical tasks. 🧵👇 Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07639 Blog: https://quantumfrontiers.com/2026/04/09/unleashing-the-advantage-of-quantum-ai/…

Haimeng Zhao

item.title: The Caltech group knows it has years of work ahead before any of its dreams have a chance of coming...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
The Caltech group knows it has years of work ahead before any of its dreams have a chance of coming true. But the researchers can’t wait to get started. “Pick a cooler life quest than building the world’s first quantum computer with your friends!” said a jubilant @DolevBluvstein

John Preskill

item.title: With extraordinary colleagues, we are developing new architectures for neutral-atom quantum processo...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
With extraordinary colleagues, we are developing new architectures for neutral-atom quantum processors that dramatically reduce the resource estimates for fault-tolerant quantum computing. This progress makes me optimistic that broadly useful quantum computing will soon be a

John Preskill

item.title: [John Preskill 已转帖] Thanks a lot to my amazing coauthors for this wonderful collaboration! @CSenrui, Marco Fanizza, Alfr...
item.author: Francesco Anna Mele
item.content:encoded:
Thanks a lot to my amazing coauthors for this wonderful collaboration! @CSenrui, Marco Fanizza, Alfred Li, Zachary Mann, Hsin-Yuan (Robert) Huang, Yanbei Chen, and @preskill 19/

Francesco Anna Mele

item.title: I'm looking forward to the Kavli Symposium tomorrow at the APS Global Physics Summit, where I'll spe...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
I'm looking forward to the Kavli Symposium tomorrow at the APS Global Physics Summit, where I'll speak about "The Road to Quantum Advantage" https://summit.aps.org/events/MAR-X02

John Preskill

item.title: I'm thrilled that Charlie Bennett and Gilles Brassard have received the A. M. Turing Award for “thei...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
I'm thrilled that Charlie Bennett and Gilles Brassard have received the A. M. Turing Award for “their essential role in establishing the foundations of quantum information science and transforming secure communication and computing.” Congratulations!

John Preskill

item.title: In this thoughtful essay, Garnet Chan reflects on recent progress using classical heuristics in comp...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
In this thoughtful essay, Garnet Chan reflects on recent progress using classical heuristics in computational quantum chemistry—and what it means for quantum computing. The lessons he draws can help to steer both classical and quantum approaches in scientifically productive

John Preskill

item.title: Today was the last meeting of our first-year seminar @Caltech: "Quantum information science today an...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
Today was the last meeting of our first-year seminar @Caltech: "Quantum information science today and tomorrow." I'm grateful to (from left to right) Andy, Jin, Fan, Ostap, Justin, Idil, Lily, Ella, and Ronald for their sparkling enthusiasm and many perceptive insights.

John Preskill

item.title: With characteristic attention to detail, @nicoleyh11 offers guidance on handling faculty offers.
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
With characteristic attention to detail, @nicoleyh11 offers guidance on handling faculty offers.

John Preskill

item.title: In the last post of a 3-part series, Dominik Hangleiter highlights the importance of efficient verif...
item.author: John Preskill
item.content:encoded:
In the last post of a 3-part series, Dominik Hangleiter highlights the importance of efficient verification of quantum advantage, and assesses the prospects for achieving it soon.

John Preskill
